A Journey to Niagara Falls
Curated for Virginia Swearingen · Early September
Early September · 6 Nights Buffalo Niagara (BUF) 2 Travelers
Begin Below
Good morning, Virginia — here is the trip we have shaped around what you shared with us. Below you will find two hotel options to choose from, a guided tour of the Falls including the famous boat ride on the river, your rental car, and a quick look at how it all comes together. Take your time and read at your own pace.

What This Proposal Covers
  • Six nights of accommodations — your choice between two well-suited Hilton properties
  • One economy rental car for the duration of your trip, picked up at the Buffalo airport
  • One guided tour of the Falls from the Canadian side — includes the boat ride on the Niagara River right up to the base of the Falls
  • A dedicated agent on call for changes, requests, and questions
  • Flight booking will be handled separately when you are ready — your vouchers remain on file
Next: Your Hotel Options
Option A

The Falls-View Choice

This is the option that delivers exactly what you asked for — seeing the Falls right from your room. The Hilton Niagara Falls/Fallsview sits on the Canadian side, directly across from Horseshoe Falls, and rooms are tiered by how much of the view you take in.

Tap the big black price bar below — it will turn green when selected.
Option A · Falls View From Your Room
Hilton Niagara Falls / Fallsview Hotel & Suites
6361 Fallsview Boulevard · Niagara Falls, ON · Canada
Falls-view room — the Falls are right outside your window
53-floor twin towers in the heart of the Fallsview district
Room Class
Falls-View, 2 Queen Beds
View Tier
U.S. Falls or Both Falls
Distance to Falls
Short walk · downhill
From U.S. Border
5 minutes · Rainbow Bridge
53-Floor Tower Indoor Pool Fitness Center 4 Restaurants Connected to Casino Watermark Fallsview Dining Starbucks On Site

Canada's tallest hotel tower, with rooms looking directly across at both the American and Canadian Horseshoe Falls. Watermark Fallsview Restaurant on level 33 has floor-to-ceiling windows over the Falls — you can plan an evening there and never leave the building. The hotel also runs a 10:00 PM nightly fireworks show right outside your window through the summer season.

Agent's note — The view is the entire point of this property, but it does come at a premium. Standard "U.S. Falls View" rooms run noticeably less than "Both Falls View" or "Premium" view rooms; we'd start you in a Falls View 2-Queen and upgrade only if you want the panoramic angle. Note: a daily resort fee (around 11.5% of the room) applies on top of the rate. The hotel is a short downhill walk to the Falls — easy on the way down, a steady climb on the way back. There is a paid trolley if needed.
Starting From · 6 Nights
☝ Tap Here to Select This Hotel ✓ Selected
from$1,770.00
~$295/night + taxes & resort fee
Next: The Buffalo Alternative
Option B

The Smart Value Choice

I want to be straight with you, Virginia — staying in Buffalo means you will not have a Falls view from your room. Buffalo is roughly 25 minutes by car from the Falls. What you gain is a true two-room suite layout that handles the "two bedrooms" request cleanly, and a meaningful savings you can put toward dining or a second excursion.

Tap the big black price bar below — it will turn green when selected.
Option B · Lowest Rate · 2-Room Suite
Embassy Suites by Hilton Buffalo
200 Delaware Avenue · Downtown Buffalo, NY
Glass-walled lobby and atrium · downtown Buffalo's Avant Building
Room Class
2-Room Suite, 2 Queen Beds
Configuration
Separate Bedroom + Living Room
Distance to Falls
~25 minutes by car
Distance to BUF Airport
~15 minutes
All-Suite Hotel Free Cooked-to-Order Breakfast Indoor Heated Pool Fitness Center Evening Reception On-Site Restaurant Walking Distance to Theater District

A downtown Buffalo property in the historic Avant Building, with two-room suites as the standard layout — a separate bedroom and a living-room area, which is the closest match to the "2 bedrooms" feel without paying for an actual two-bedroom suite. Cooked-to-order breakfast is included every morning, and there is a complimentary evening reception with light bites and drinks.

Agent's note — If you choose this option, plan on driving to the Falls each day. The drive is straightforward and traffic at the border is rarely heavy outside of summer holiday weekends. The savings versus the Fallsview Hilton are real — you would be paying for a hotel as lodging, not as the view. Also worth noting: this hotel is in a walkable downtown with dining and theater nearby, which the Fallsview hotel district does not really offer.
Starting From · 6 Nights
☝ Tap Here to Select This Hotel ✓ Selected
from$1,194.00
~$199/night + taxes
Next: Explore More
Explore More

Tours, Dining & Experiences

Here are a few additional experiences I'd recommend for your time in Niagara. Tap any that interest you — they will be added to your selections, and we'll firm them up when we finalize the booking.
Featured Excursion — Guided Tour with the Boat Ride
Walk Niagara Adventure Tour Recommended
A guided walking tour of the Falls with a local expert, then the boat ride on the Niagara River right up to the base of Horseshoe Falls (this is the famous one — ponchos provided), plus Journey Behind the Falls where you go down inside the rock to see the Falls from behind · About 3 hours total · ~$110/pp
Skylon Tower Observation Deck
775-ft tower view of both Falls · ~$20/pp · Pair with dinner at the Revolving Dining Room (~$60–80/pp)
Niagara-on-the-Lake Wine Country Day Trip
25 min drive · Historic town + 2–3 winery visits · ~$25–40/pp tasting fees
Dining — Niagara Falls Canada Side
Watermark Fallsview Restaurant
Inside the Hilton Fallsview · Floor-to-ceiling Falls views from level 33 · ~$65–95/pp dinner
Table Rock House Restaurant
Right at the brink of Horseshoe Falls · Canadian regional menu · ~$50–75/pp
Dining — Buffalo (If Choosing Option B)
Anchor Bar (Original)
Birthplace of the Buffalo wing · Casual, iconic · ~$20–30/pp
Oliver's Restaurant
Buffalo institution · Continental fine dining · ~$55–85/pp
